reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by acting as a malicious proposer and sending two DataColumnSidecars for (slot=N, proposer=V, index=I) with different content and valid signatures, causing both to pass validation and be stored under different blockRootHex keys. This bypasses the anti-equivocation check (spec requirement #12) because the cache keys by blockRootHex instead of (slot, proposer, index), enabling equivocation attacks that undermine PeerDAS data availability guarantees.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Spec requirement #12: anti-equivocation check for data columns
